title: NOC 40042 – Commissioned Officers of the Canadian Armed Forces | GTR Immigration
description: Commissioned Officers of the Canadian Armed Forces under NOC 40042 are senior military leaders responsible for planning, commanding, and managing strategic operations within Canadian Armed Forces across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents top-tier leadership within Canada’s national defense and public protection framework. These officers serve in the Army, the Royal Canadian Air Force, and the Royal Canadian Navy, both domestically and internationally.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration and public protection servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and protection servicesMinor Group4004 – Managers in public protection servicesNOC Code40042

duties: Commissioned Officers perform some or all of the following:
Allocate material, human, and financial resources to implement military policies
Establish administrative controls and personnel planning
Plan, organize, and command combat and strategic units
Direct disaster relief, humanitarian, and peacekeeping missions
Set organizational objectives aligned with federal legislation
Coordinate operations among military strategic units
Develop and implement military procedures and policies
employment_requirements: Experience as a non-commissioned member may substitute for degree in some cases
Several years of experience required for senior ranks (e.g., Colonel, Brigadier General)
Commissioned Officers must meet federal eligibility criteria established by the Canadian Armed Forces, including citizenship requirements and security clearance.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $50,900 to $113,100, depending on rank and years of service.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 40042 is stable, driven by:
However, recruitment is directly controlled by federal defense authorities.
labour_market_demand: Employment opportunities are tied directly to federal defense planning and recruitment initiatives within the Canadian Armed Forces.
However, Commissioned Officer positions typically require Canadian citizenship and direct recruitment through Canada’s federal military system.
pathways: While NOC 40042 is classified under TEER 0, most Commissioned Officer roles require Canadian citizenship and enlistment through the national military system.
If you are an experienced military professional seeking permanent residence in Canada, alternative immigration pathways may include:
Express Entry (if eligible under civilian equivalents)
Employer-supported pathways in related civilian occupations
Contact GTR Immigration to evaluate the most suitable immigration strategy based on your military and leadership background.
